title: Duke Energy (DUK)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Duke Energy using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what Duke Energy is really worth.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-cksdilyf>
With the 10Y Treasury at 4.18%, a 7% required return is standard for regulated utilities given their low volatility and predictable cash flows. A higher discount rate would be overly punitive for a monopoly business.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-cksdilyf>
Long-term nominal GDP growth is around 2-3%. A 2% perpetuity growth rate assumes Duke Energy will grow slightly below GDP over the long run, simply keeping pace with inflation and population growth in its territories.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use an adjusted Operating Cash Flow instead of Free Cash Flow?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "Duke Energy (DUK) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.
